tools.name: convert
tools.description: Convert between different measurement units. Supports length, weight, temperature, area, volume, speed, time, and data units. Use this for any unit conversion between metric, imperial, and digital units. For listing available units or seeing categories, use list_units, categories, or help instead.

tools.name: list_units
tools.description: List all available units in a specific category. Use this to see which units are supported for conversion in a given category. For performing actual conversions, use convert instead.

category.description: The category to list units for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

tools.name: categories
tools.description: Show all supported unit conversion categories. Use this to discover what types of conversions are available. For listing units within a specific category, use list_units instead.

tools.name: help
tools.description: Show detailed help and information for a specific unit category. Use this to learn about the units and conversion formulas available for a category. For listing all categories, use categories instead.

category.description: The category to show help for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

messages.content: Hey, I'm helping a college football player with their training measurements and I was wondering what kinds of unit conversions you can help with? We deal with speed, weight, distance - all that kind of stuff for training purposes.
I'd be happy to help with your training measurements! Let me show you what conversion categories are available.

Great news! I can help with exactly the kinds of measurements you'd need for football training. Here are the available categories:

| Category | Training Applications |
|----------|----------------------|
| **Length** | Distances for sprints, field dimensions, measurement of equipment |
| **Weight** | Player weight, strength training (plates, barbells) |
| **Speed** | 40-yard dash times, sprint speed (mph, km/h, m/s) |
| **Time** | Drill timing, split times, workout intervals |
| **Volume** | Hydration, fluid intake |
| **Temperature** | Weather/conditioning monitoring |
| **Area** | Field area calculations |
| **Data** | Probably less relevant for on-field training! |

So whether you need to convert:
- **Speed**: 40-yard dash times to mph or m/s
- **Weight**: Body weight or weights between pounds and kilograms
- **Distance**: Yards to meters for training benchmarks
- **Time**: Minutes:seconds to decimal minutes for timing logs

I've got you covered! Just let me know what specific conversions you need, and I'll handle them for you.

That's helpful! So for football specifically, when we're talking about speed - like a player's 40-yard dash time or their sprint speed - what are the typical units we'd be working with? I want to make sure I'm using the right measurements when comparing times to what they see in training logs or on TV.
Good question! Let me show you what speed units are available so you know exactly what you're working with.

{"status": "success", "category": "speed", "units": [{"name": "meters per second", "symbol": "m/s"}, {"name": "kilometers per hour", "symbol": "km/h"}, {"name": "miles per hour", "symbol": "mph"}, {"name": "feet per second", "symbol": "ft/s"}, {"name": "knots", "symbol": "kn"}, {"name": "mach", "symbol": "Ma"}, {"name": "speed of light", "symbol": "c"}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here's what you're working with for football speed measurements:

| Unit | Symbol | When You'll See It |
|------|--------|-------------------|
| **Miles per hour** | mph | Most common in the US - NFL Combine, training logs, TV broadcasts |
| **Kilometers per hour** | km/h | Common internationally, some modern tracking tech |
| **Meters per second** | m/s | Scientific/research contexts, some GPS tracking systems |
| **Feet per second** | ft/s | Occasionally used in older timing systems |

The big one for football is **mph** - that's what the Combine uses for 40-yard dash "official" speeds, what you'll see on sports broadcasts, and what most training apps log.

If you're comparing a player's 40-yard dash to what they see on TV or in a training report, you're almost certainly looking at **mph**. 

Want me to show you how to convert between any of these? For example, say you have a 4.4-second 40-yard dash and want to know what that is in mph - I can walk through that calculation with you!
That would be perfect! One of our players ran a 4.5-second 40-yard dash at practice last week. Can you convert that to km/h so we can compare it to some European training data we've been looking at?

Let me calculate that for you! First I need to figure out the speed in meters per second, then convert to km/h.

A 4.5-second 40-yard dash works out to:
- 40 yards = 36.576 meters
- Speed = 36.576 m ÷ 4.5 s = **8.13 m/s**

Now let me convert that to km/h:

Here's the breakdown:

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| 40-yard dash time | 4.5 seconds |
| Speed in m/s | 8.13 m/s |
| **Speed in km/h** | **29.27 km/h** |

So your player's time compares to about **29.3 km/h** in the European training data.

For reference, that's roughly **18.2 mph** - which is a solid time for a college player. Elite NFL receivers and cornerbacks typically run in the 20-22 mph range, so your player is in good company with room to grow!

Now that we've got the speed conversion sorted, I need to figure out some weight conversions too. One of our players is about 85 kilograms and I want to convert that to pounds so we can set up his barbell weights correctly - most of our plates are labeled in lbs.

Here's the conversion:

| Weight |
|--------|
| 85 kg = **187.4 lbs** |

So when you're setting up his barbell, you'll be working with around **187 lbs** total (body weight + bar).

For his strength training, that means:
- A typical barbell is 45 lbs
- If he's lifting his bodyweight, he'd load about **142 lbs** on the bar (187 - 45 = 142)
- That's roughly two 45-lb plates per side, plus the bar

Want me to help with any other conversions - maybe distance for sprint distances or something else for his training?

Perfect timing! Yeah, we actually do need a distance conversion. We're setting up some shuttle run drills and the field markings are in yards, but one of our European coaches sent over a training protocol that uses meters. Can you convert something like 60 yards to meters for us?

Here's your conversion:

| Distance |
|----------|
| 60 yards = **54.86 meters** |

That's a very common shuttle run distance! The 60-yard shuttle (sometimes called the "5-10-5" or "pro shuttle") is one of the standard drills at the Combine and for position testing.

So when your European coach's training protocol calls for a 60m shuttle drill, you'll set up your markings at about **55 yards** on the field.

Want me to convert any other distances for the drill setups?
